description:
# Open question

Commit message k:v parser rules.

- Exact format: blank-line-separated sections (subject, k:v block, free-form body). Where is the k:v block allowed to live — only between subject and body, or anywhere?
- Reserved keys: `op`, `ball`, `actor`, `from-state`, `to-state`, `protocol`. Are these required on every commit, or only when relevant?
- Plugin extensions: namespacing convention for plugin-added keys (`jira-id`, `github-url`)? Or just lower-kebab?
- Multi-ball ops: `ball: bl-1234, bl-5678` comma-separated, or repeated lines `ball: bl-1234\nball: bl-5678`?
- Unknown keys: passed through to plugins or dropped?
title: Topic: commit message k:v protocol
